text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Daniel Morgan, DOB: 22-09-1984, Hospital No: U914275 NHS No. 1045829376  
42 Alder Grove, London, NW3 5QH 

Summary/Diagnoses: Focal epilepsy following presumed left temporal onset/previous head injury in adolescence/mild anxiety symptoms/ongoing tobacco use/past variability in pharmacy supply with intermittent adherence issues (historical). 

Medication: Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.  
Vitamin D3 800 IU once daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg once daily.  

I reviewed Daniel Morgan in clinic today, attending alone. He reports feeling generally well and is engaging with his routine activities, including part-time volunteering at a local charity shop. Sleep has improved with a more consistent schedule and reduced late-night screen time. Appetite is stable and weight unchanged. He continues to smoke approximately 5–7 cigarettes per day and is contemplating cessation support. 

Seizures: He describes focal epilepsy with awareness preserved during brief auras of rising epigastric sensation, occasionally followed by right-hand automatisms and speech arrest lasting 30–60 seconds. According to his personal seizure diary and phone reminders, he experienced a cluster of short focal events in the latter part of last month, with no generalised tonic–clonic activity. Prior to this, he had a six-week period without events. He links the recent cluster to a late refill caused by pharmacy stock issues, during which he missed two evening doses of levetiracetam; supply has since normalised and he reports full adherence over the past three weeks. There is no new head injury, intercurrent infection, or sleep deprivation beyond one late night preceding the cluster. No injuries, tongue biting, or incontinence reported. Post-event fatigue is mild and resolves within an hour. 

Adherence and supply: Historic variability in pharmacy provision is documented. He has now set up electronic repeat dispensing with his community pharmacy and uses a weekly pill organiser. He has also opted into text alerts from the pharmacy to prompt timely collection. He understands the importance of dose regularity. 

Examination: Alert and oriented, normal speech between events. Cranial nerves intact. Motor examination normal tone and power throughout. Sensation grossly intact. Coordination normal. Gait steady. No focal neurological deficits elicited today. 

Investigations: Last serum lamotrigine level (four months ago) within therapeutic range; routine bloods including FBC, U&E, LFTs normal. MRI brain (2023) with left mesial temporal sclerosis features unchanged from prior imaging. 

Plan:  
- Continue lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily and levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily without change.  
- Reinforce adherence strategies; continue electronic repeat dispensing and pharmacy text alerts.  
- Seizure diary to be maintained; if further clustering occurs despite reliable supply, consider levetiracetam up-titration to 1 g twice daily or introduction of lacosamide—will review at next appointment.  
- Safety counselling updated (driving advice remains not applicable as he is not currently driving; kitchen and bath safety discussed).  
- Check routine bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs, vitamin D) before next visit.  
- Follow-up in 4 months, earlier if breakthrough events recur.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: experienced a cluster of short focal events in the latter part of last month

attributes.Label: 1 cluster per month, multiple per cluster

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis:
- Clinic date: 02 Oct 2025.
- Letter states: “he experienced a cluster of short focal events in the latter part of last month,” implying a single cluster occurred in September 2025.
- Prior period: six weeks seizure-free; this does not meet the 6-month seizure-free criterion, so not seizure free.
- No exact count of seizures in the cluster is given, only that it was a cluster (i.e., multiple events).
- Applying the rule to choose the highest frequency and use explicit statements, the clearest current frequency is 1 cluster in the last month with an unspecified (multiple) number of seizures within that cluster.
seizure_frequency_number: 1 cluster per month, multiple per cluster
experienced a cluster of short focal events in the latter part of last month

flaws.description: Gold (1 cluster per month, multiple per cluster) disagrees with Reference (multiple per month)
